findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is unconditionally true by Python identity semantics. This test cannot fail under any code change and covers nothing about Settings behaviour.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful identity or value assertion, e.g. `assert isinstance(s, Settings)` to verify the constructor returns the right type, or remove the test entirely and rely on the existing `test_defaults`.

findings.message: Over-mocked: `mpatch.object` replaces the exact property under test (`resolved_log_level`) with a stub, then asserts the stub returns its own hardcoded value. The assertion is only a proof that `unittest.mock.patch.object` works; it says nothing about the real implementation. The test will pass regardless of how `resolved_log_level` is implemented or whether it is deleted.
findings.suggestion: Remove this test — the property's logic is already fully covered by `test_resolved_log_level_is_debug_in_dev`, `test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev`, and `test_explicit_log_level_overrides_resolution`. If a new branch is needed, call `Settings(...)` directly with the relevant inputs and assert the real return value.
